rationale: Both reach the same plausible technical diagnosis (PR #4834's intentional NotImplementedError guard vs. a stale test still calling sleep(level=2)+wake_up), but the actual thread shows this is unresolved — Flink-ddd is asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode run to investigate, not confirming a known fix — so both candidates' confident 'already fixed on main, close' verdict overshoots what the thread established, and both invent unverifiable line numbers/test names beyond what's in the issue body. Y is marginally better calibrated: it flags its own confidence explicitly, hedges that the fix may not be present at the reporter's commit ('if CI is still failing... cherry-pick or rebase'), and stops short of X's more elaborate fabricated specifics (e.g. an invented 'model-adaptation-review skill' checklist).
